“Care Instructions”, “Dry Clean Only”, “Hand Wash Only”, “Machine Wash”, “Tumble Dry”, “Washable”; product cards show “King Size”, “Memory Foam”, “Options: 3 sizes”, ratings/prices.
selected_candidate:
No product selected yet; likely the “Washable” filter on the left sidebar.
constraint_evidence:
- bamboo material: not_visible
- memory foam: supported (title/url context and visible product cards include memory foam pillows)
- pillow: supported (search results are pillows)
- washable cover: supported for filter intent only via visible “Washable” care instruction; not yet confirmed on a specific product
- king size: supported (URL/title context and visible “King Size” on results)
- available on Amazon: supported (Amazon results page)
- specific product match: not_visible

success_signals:
Filter becomes selected/applied; results update to show washable pillows, ideally still king size and memory foam.
failure_signals:
No filter change; wrong filter toggled; results narrow away from king size or memory foam; page navigates unexpectedly.
uncertainty:
The screenshot does not show whether any listed product explicitly has a washable cover or bamboo material; only the filter opt</TRAJECTA_DATA_2dabbaa4>

args.query: Amazon shopping task ended on product page where bamboo memory foam pillow and king size are visible, but required washable cover was never explicitly verified; agent relied on search filter only

items.task: find: answer to 'Does it come with a warranty?'
items.expected_behavior: The agent should have used the visible Warranty & Support information and answered in line with it, rather than claiming the item does not come with a warranty.
items.actual_behavior: The agent answered that the item does not come with a warranty even though the page showed a Warranty & Support section with a manufacturer warranty note.

evidence.claim: The task was to answer whether the Ello Syndicate 20oz reusable glass water bottle comes with a warranty.
Screenshot 8 shows the Amazon product page scrolled to the Warranty & Support section, where warranty information is visible.

The visible text on screenshot 8 includes 'Warranty & Support' and 'Manufacturer’s warranty can be requested from customer service,' showing that warranty-related information was available on the page.
The agent nonetheless answered '[ANSWER] It doesn't come with a warranty.' at the final step, which conflicts with the warranty information visible on screenshot 8.

items.regression_rule: When a page visibly contains warranty/support information, the agent must not answer 'no warranty' unless the visible text explicitly says there is no warranty; if the warranty terms are vague, answer with the available warranty wording instead.

items.task: navigate: Search for a hotel in Kyoto with a user rating of 7 or higher, equipped with a kitchenette, available for a stay from April 5 to April 8, 2026.
items.expected_behavior: The agent should have correctly set the dates to April 5-8, 2026, and then selected a hotel in Kyoto that met the rating (7+) and amenity (kitchenette) requirements.
items.actual_behavior: The agent searched for hotels in Kyoto with a rating of 7+ and a kitchenette, but it failed to set the correct dates (April 5-8, 2026). The search results were for different dates, and the agent exited without selecting a hotel that met all the criteria.

evidence.claim: The search bar shows dates 'Wed, Feb 25 — Thu, Feb 26' instead of the requested 'April 5 to April 8, 2026'.

The agent failed to set the correct dates (April 5-8, 2026) in the search parameters, resulting in incorrect availability results. The agent is exiting prematurely without selecting a hotel that meets all criteria.

items.regression_rule: Ensure that all date and amenity constraints are correctly applied in the search form before proceeding to select a result.

items.task: find: answer to 'How many of these were submitted by authors affiliated with institutions outside the USA?'
items.expected_behavior: The agent should have attempted to navigate to individual paper pages or use other available tools/methods to determine the author affiliations for the papers in the search results, or at least attempted a representative sample if the full set was too large.
items.actual_behavior: The agent performed an [EXIT] action immediately after loading the search results page, without attempting to investigate the author affiliations of the papers.

evidence.claim: The agent did not attempt to access individual paper pages or any other source to determine author affiliations.
The task requires checking author affiliations for a large number of papers, which is not possible from the search results page alone. The agent exited without attempting any further steps.

items.regression_rule: The agent must not exit a task prematurely without attempting to gather the necessary information to answer the user's query, especially when the initial page does not contain the required data.

args.expected_behavior: After narrowing results to king size, memory foam, bamboo, and washable-related filters, the agent should verify on the chosen product page that the product explicitly has a washable cover before exiting.
args.actual_behavior: The agent opened a bamboo memory foam king-size pillow product page and exited, but the final visible product page does not explicitly confirm that the cover is washable; washable was only represented as a search filter earlier, not verified on the selected product.
args.regression_rule: For shopping tasks with explicit product attributes, do not treat search-filter state alone as proof that the chosen item satisfies the attribute; before exiting, verify the selected product page visibly confirms each required attribute, especially the final missing qualifier.

evidence.claim: The task is to find a bamboo memory foam pillow on Amazon with a washable cover and available in king size.

On the results page, the agent targeted the left-side 'Washable' filter under Care Instructions. This supports only filter intent; the high-detail read says washable cover is not yet confirmed on a specific product.

Before opening the item, the selected result card visibly supports memory foam, and the search context supports king size, but the high-detail read marks bamboo as not visible on the card and does not explicitly show king size on the card itself.
On the final product page, the title visibly shows a memory foam pillow with bamboo cover and king size, but the high-detail constraint evidence marks 'washable cover' as not visible.
Validated precedent: a task with explicit hard constraints was labeled missed_constraint when the agent progressed but failed to verify the required constraint before exiting.

result.task: navigate: Find a bamboo memory foam pillow on Amazon with a washable cover and available in king size.
result.expected_behavior: After narrowing results to king size, memory foam, bamboo, and washable-related filters, the agent should verify on the chosen product page that the product explicitly has a washable cover before exiting.
result.actual_behavior: The agent opened a bamboo memory foam king-size pillow product page and exited, but the final visible product page does not explicitly confirm that the cover is washable; washable was only represented as a search filter earlier, not verified on the selected product.

evidence.claim: The task is to find a bamboo memory foam pillow on Amazon with a washable cover and available in king size.

On the results page, the agent targeted the left-side 'Washable' filter under Care Instructions. This supports only filter intent; the high-detail read says washable cover is not yet confirmed on a specific product.

Before opening the item, the selected result card visibly supports memory foam, and the search context supports king size, but the high-detail read marks bamboo as not visible on the card and does not explicitly show king size on the card itself.
On the final product page, the title visibly shows a memory foam pillow with bamboo cover and king size, but the high-detail constraint evidence marks 'washable cover' as not visible.
Validated precedent: a task with explicit hard constraints was labeled missed_constraint when the agent progressed but failed to verify the required constraint before exiting.

result.regression_rule: For shopping tasks with explicit product attributes, do not treat search-filter state alone as proof that the chosen item satisfies the attribute; before exiting, verify the selected product page visibly confirms each required attribute, especially the final missing qualifier.
